2603.29901.title: Less Is More? Selective Visual Attention to High-Importance Regions for Multimodal Radiology Summarization
2603.29901.paper_summary: Automated radiology report summarization aims to distill verbose findings into concise clinical impressions, but existing multimodal models often struggle with visual noise and fail to meaningfully improve over strong text-only baselines in the FINDINGS $\to$ IMPRESSION transformation. We challenge two prevailing assumptions: (1) that more visual input is always better, and (2) that multimodal models add limited value when findings already contain rich image-derived detail. Through controlled ablations on MIMIC-CXR benchmark, we show that selectively focusing on pathology-relevant visual patches rather than full images yields substantially better performance. We introduce ViTAS, Visual-Text Attention Summarizer, a multi-stage pipeline that combines ensemble-guided MedSAM2 lung segmentation, bidirectional cross-attention for multi-view fusion, Shapley-guided adaptive patch clustering, and hierarchical visual tokenization feeding a ViT. ViTAS achieves SOTA results with 29.25% BLEU-4 and 69.83% ROUGE-L, improved factual alignment in qualitative analysis, and the highest expert-rated human evaluation scores. Our findings demonstrate that less but more relevant visual input is not only sufficient but superior for multimodal radiology summarization.
2603.29901.paper_summary_zh: 自動化放射學報告摘要的目標是將冗長的發現提煉成簡潔的臨床印象，但現有的多模態模型往往在視覺噪聲方面掙扎，並且在 FINDINGS $\to$ IMPRESSION 轉換中未能顯著改善強文本基線。我們挑戰兩個普遍的假設：（1）更多的視覺輸入總是更好，以及（2）當發現已經包含豐富的圖像衍生細節時，多模態模型的附加價值有限。通過對 MIMIC-CXR 基準的受控消融實驗，我們顯示出選擇性地專注於與病理相關的視覺區塊，而不是完整圖像，能夠顯著提高性能。我們介紹了 ViTAS，視覺-文本注意力摘要器，一個多階段管道，結合了集成引導的 MedSAM2 肺部分分割、雙向交叉注意力進行多視角融合、Shapley 引導的自適應區塊聚類，以及層次視覺標記化，為 ViT 提供輸入。ViTAS 在 BLEU-4 方面達到 29.25% 和 ROUGE-L 方面達到 69.83% 的 SOTA 結果，在定性分析中改善了事實對齊，並獲得了最高的專家評分人類評估分數。我們的研究結果表明，較少但更相關的視覺輸入不僅足夠，還對多模態放射學摘要具有優越性。
2603.29901.author: Mst. Fahmida Sultana Naznin et.al.
2603.29901.authors: Mst. Fahmida Sultana Naznin, Adnan Ibney Faruq, Mushfiqur Rahman, Niloy Kumar Mondal, Md. Mehedi Hasan Shawon, Md Rakibul Hasan
